LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF VIRAJ PATEL'S MOTION TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT TO ALLEGE OPPRESSION AND MALICE AND TO REQUEST PUNITIVE DAMAGES AS AGAINST DEFENDANT K.J. WOODS CONSTRUCTION, INC. ONLY IS GRANTED. MR. PATEL IS GRANTED LEAVE TO FILE HIS PROPOSED FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT THAT IS INCLUDED IN HIS MOVING PAPERS NO LATER THAN DECEMBER 12, 2017. GRANTING THE MOTION COMPORTS WITH CALIFORNIA'S LIBERAL POLICY OF ALLOWING AMENDMENTS TO PLEADINGS AND K.J. WOODS HAS NOT IDENTIFIED ANY LEGAL PREJUDICE THAT IT WOULD SUFFER BY ALLOWING MR. PATEL TO SEEK PUNITIVE DAMAGES. WHILE THE COURT IS NOT PRECLUDING K.J. WOODS FROM FILING A MOTION TO STRIKE THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES ALLEGATIONS IN THE PROPOSED FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, IT IS VERY LIKELY THAT THE MOTION WOULD BE DENIED. LIBERALLY CONSTRUING THE PROPOSED FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, IT APPEARS THAT THAT PLEADING ADEQUATELY ALLEGES THAT K.J. WOODS KNEW THAT MR. O'NEILL LACKED THE ABILITY TO SAFELY OPERATE THE FORKLIFT AND THAT, ABSENT TAKING APPROPRIATE PRECAUTIONS SUCH AS HAVING A FLAGMAN, ALLOWING MR. O'NEILL TO OPERATE THE FORKLIFT IN THE MANNER HE DID POSED A SERIOUS RISK OF HARM TO OTHERS WHO WERE IN THE VICINITY OF HIS FORKLIFT. THESE ALLEGATIONS APPEAR TO SHOW THAT K.J. WOODS ACTED DESPICABLY AND WITH A WILLFUL AND CONSCIOUS DISREGARD OF THE RIGHTS AND SAFETY OF OTHERS. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312.JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: ANN DELGADO, CSR NO. 11185, E-MAIL: ADELGADO@SFTC.ORG. (L&M302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Defense Investigator Tava Miyata With Production Of Documents Duces Tecum
Off Calendar Oct-18-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Atty. Gregory J. O'Connell of RLS, PC, Tel. No. 1-415-341-9341 appearing for the
Plaintiff;
Atty. Charles S. Custer, Tel. No. 1-415-986-5900 appearing for the Defendants.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF VIRAJ PATEL's Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Defense Investigator Tava Miyata With Production Of Documents Duces Tecum - As is common in personal injury litigation, defendants' counsel surveilled plaintiff, who is said to have lost his sight in a motor vehicle accident. Plaintiff seeks to compel a deposition of the investigator as well as surveillance photos, videos and/or reports and interrogatory answers regarding the surveillance. The attorney-client privilege and attorney work product doctrine are at issue.
Plaintiff says the investigator is "an employee of defense counsel" - an assertion not helpful to plaintiff, given the attorney-client privilege's breadth. Actually, the investigator is more appropriately viewed - as defendants do - as an expert consultant who may or may not testify at trial. Such consultants are not subject to deposition unless and until they are designated trial witnesses. (See Williamson v. Sup. Ct. (1978) 21 Cal.3d 829, 834-35.)
The surveillance photos, videos and/or reports are a closer call. In Coito v. Sup. Ct. (2012) 54 Cal.4th 480, 486, our supreme court held that recorded witness statements are entitled to protection under the work product doctrine. I find the Coito logic to apply to surveillance recordings and reports too, because they may "reveal'attorney's impressions, conclusions, opinions, or legal research or theories." (See id.) (The opinion that plaintiff relies on - Suezaki v. Sup. Ct. (1962) 58 Cal.2d 166 - is less persuasive, as it pre-dates modern discovery law.)
Coito calls for a finding that counsel's work product would be revealed. (54 Cal. 4th at 486.) The court ordered the submission on or before October 25, 2018, of any and all surveillance photos, videos and reports for my in camera review. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600 =302/ru

MOTION - Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Venue To Contra Costa County Or In The Alternative, Santa Clara
Off Calendar Sep-07-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance by any other party or counsel.

DEFENDANTS K.J. WOODS CONSTRUCTION, INC. AND BARRY EDWARD O'NEILL'S MOTION TO TRANSFER VENUE TO CONTRA COSTA COUNTY OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SANTA CLARA.

There being no other appearance, the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Sixteen months after answering the complaint and two months before trial, defendants move to transfer venue under CCP 397(c). The motion is DENIED, first because it is much-belated. (See, e.g., Willingham v. Pecora (1941) 44 Cal.App.2d 289, 294-95; Thompson v. Sup. Ct. (1972) 26 Cal.App.3d 300, 309-10.) Second, witnesses are spread among five counties in the San Francisco Bay Area, where the San Francisco Superior Court is centrally and conveniently located. (See CCP 397(c).) Order signed. (see order for full details)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend The Complaint To Allege Oppression And Malice And To Request Punitive Damages As Against Defendant K.J. Woods Construction, Inc. Only
Off Calendar Dec-05-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, E. Robert Wallach of the law offices of Rains Lucia Stern, PC (415-341-9341) appears as counsel for plaintiff/moving party

Attorney, Ruth D. Kahn of the law offices of John A. Hauser (213-443-9063) appears by telephone as counsel for defendant/opposing party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF VIRAJ PATEL'S Motion To Amend The Complaint To Allege Oppression And Malice And To Request Punitive Damages As Against Defendant K.J. Woods Construction, Inc. Only is GRANTED. Mr. Patel is granted leave to file his proposed first amended complaint that is included in his moving papers no later than December 12, 2017. Granting the motion comports with California's liberal policy of allowing amendments to pleadings and K.J. Woods has not identified any legal prejudice that it would suffer by allowing Mr. Patel to seek punitive damages. While the court is not precluding K.J. Woods from filing a motion to strike the punitive damages allegations in the proposed first amended complaint, it is very likely that the motion would be denied. Liberally construing the proposed first amended complaint, it appears that that pleading adequately alleges that K.J. Woods knew that Mr. O'Neill lacked the ability to safely operate the forklift and that, absent taking appropriate precautions such as having a flagman, allowing Mr. O'Neill to operate the forklift in the manner he did posed a serious risk of harm to others who were in the vicinity of his forklift. These allegations appear to show that K.J. Woods acted despicably and with a willful and conscious disregard of the rights and safety of others. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR No. 11185, e-mail: adelgado@sftc.org. (L&M302)
